steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes how the author credited her inspiration to Elease Southerland's work — specifically, the work was one of her favorite books as a child. The blank must express a positive personal connection to that work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'an affinity for': affinity means a natural liking or attraction. Crediting one's inspiration to an affinity for a beloved childhood book fits precisely — it names a warm personal connection. ✓
  • Choice B — 'a stipulation about': stipulation means a condition or requirement. The presenter is not placing a requirement on Southerland's work; she is expressing how it inspired her. ✗
  • Choice C — 'an ambivalence toward': ambivalence means mixed or conflicting feelings. A book listed as among one's favorites as a child cannot be something one feels ambivalent about. ✗
  • Choice D — 'a complacency about': complacency means smug self-satisfaction or uncritical acceptance. This describes an attitude of indifference, not the positive inspiration the passage conveys. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
